draft.introduction: Luke 22:14–38 presents the Last Supper, where Jesus institutes the eucharistic meal and predicts his betrayal. Luke’s account includes unique material about servant leadership among the disciples.

translation.text: And when the hour came, he reclined at table, and the apostles with him. And he said, ‘I have earnestly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer. For I tell you I will not eat it until it is fulfilled in the kingdom of God.’ And he took bread, and gave thanks and broke it and gave it to them, saying, ‘This is my body, which is given for you.’
translation.original_text: καὶ ὅτε ἐγένετο ἡ ὥρα ἀνέπεσεν καὶ οἱ ἀπόστολοι σὺν αὐτῷ

translator_notes.note: The longer text of Luke’s institution narrative (including “which is given for you” and the cup saying) is present in Sinaiticus but absent from some Western manuscripts. This is one of the most significant textual disputes in the Gospels.
